name: Fruit-Filled Puffs
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F. Spray a large baking sheet with nonstick cooking spray; set aside.
2. In a medium saucepan, combine 1 cup water, the oil, and vanilla; bring to a boil. Stir in matzo cake meal and salt; continue cooking, stirring constantly, about 1 minute, until mixture no longer sticks to side of saucepan. Remove from heat and stir in eggs, 1 at a time, stirring thoroughly after each addition.
3. Drop dough by 12 rounded tablespoonfuls, about 2 inches apart, onto prepared baking sheet. Bake for 10 minutes. Reduce heat to 400°F and bake an additional 10-15 minutes, until browned and puffed. Cool completely on rack, then slice in half horizontally.
4. To fill, fit a pastry bag with 1/4-inch diameter star tip. Fill bag with whipped topping. Divide fruit evenly among bottom halves of puffs. Pipe or spoon 2 tablespoons topping over each filled half. Place tops on puffs and drizzle 1/2 teaspoon chocolate syrup over each.
